task_completion.description: Open Panoply and load the Sea Level Pressure dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, and the dataset 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' is visible in the Datasets tab.

task_completion.description: Create a map plot of the Sea Level Pressure for July.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply map plot window is open showing the 'slp' variable. The time/date control at the top of the plot window must be set to index 6 or explicitly show 'July'.

task_completion.description: Save the plot as 'indian_ocean_slp_july.png' in the MaritimeRouting directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in Panoply, a file manager window, or a terminal showing the file 'indian_ocean_slp_july.png' located inside the '~/Documents/MaritimeRouting/' directory.

task_completion.description: Identify the Mascarene High and Asian/Monsoon Low and compute the gradient in a report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing the contents of the report. The text should mention the Mascarene High (approx 1025.7 mb), the Asian/Monsoon Low (approx 996.0 mb), and the computed cross-equatorial gradient (approx 29.7 mb).

task_completion.description: Save the structured report as 'monsoon_gradient_report.txt' in the MaritimeRouting directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window, text editor save dialog, or terminal showing 'monsoon_gradient_report.txt' saved in the '~/Documents/MaritimeRouting/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to generate the plot and inspect the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Panoply graphical interface being actively used (e.g., adjusting plot settings, using the data probe/cursor to read pressure values), rather than a terminal running a Python script to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The agent extracted real values from the dataset rather than hallucinating.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains specific pressure values that match the dataset (High ~1025.7 mb, Low ~996.0 mb), and there is evidence of the agent reading these values in Panoply (e.g., hovering over the map or viewing the Array tab).

integrity.description: The agent saved the files to the exact requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths for the saved PNG and TXT files strictly include 'Documents/MaritimeRouting' and are not just dumped on the Desktop or in the home directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset is located at ~/PanoplyData/slp.mon.ltm.nc. The correct time index for July is 6 (0-indexed). The expected Mascarene High peak pressure in the Indian Ocean is approximately 1025.7 mb, and the Asian/Monsoon Low minimum pressure is approximately 996.0 mb, resulting in a cross-equatorial gradient of roughly 29.7 mb. The expected outputs are 'indian_ocean_slp_july.png' and 'monsoon_gradient_report.txt' saved exactly to the '~/Documents/MaritimeRouting/' directory.
